memberdef.argsstring: (XDp *InstancePtr, u16 DeviceId, INTC *IntcPtr, u16 IntrId, u16 DpIntrId, XTmrCtr *TimerCounterPtr, u16 TimerId)
memberdef.name: Dprx_SstExample

briefdescription.para: The main entry point for the interrupt with timer example using the XDp driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function will set up the system, interrupt controller and interrupt handlers, and the custom sleep handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort RX core instance.
is a pointer to the interrupt instance.
is the unique device ID of the interrupt controller.
is the interrupt ID of the DisplayPort RX connection to the interrupt controller.
is a pointer to the timer instance.
is the ID of the timer controller to use for delays.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the system was set up correctly and link training was successful.

briefdescription.para: This function will setup and initialize the DisplayPort RX core.

detaileddescription.para: The core's configuration parameters will be retrieved based on the configuration to the DisplayPort RX core instance with the specified device ID.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort RX core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the device configuration was found and obtained and if the main link was successfully established.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up a custom timer which the driver will use for MicroBlaze systems.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is a pointer to the timer instance.
is the ID of the timer controller to use for delays.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if a timer controller exists for use.

simplesect.para: A timer controller must be present in the system.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up the interrupt system such that interrupts caused by Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) events and pulses are handled.

detaileddescription.para: This function is application-specific for systems that have an interrupt controller connected to the processor. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is a pointer to the interrupt instance.
is the unique device ID of the interrupt controller.
is the interrupt ID of the DisplayPort RX connection to the interrupt controller.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the interrupt system was successfully set up.

simplesect.para: An interrupt controller must be present in the system, connected to the processor and the DisplayPort RX core.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to override the driver's default sleep functionality.

detaileddescription.para: For MicroBlaze systems, the XDp_WaitUs driver function's default behavior is to use the MB_Sleep function from microblaze_sleep.h, which is implemented in software and only has millisecond accuracy. For this reason, using a hardware timer is preferable. For ARM/Zynq SoC systems, the SoC's timer is used - XDp_WaitUs will ignore this custom timer handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDp_SetUserTimerHandler driver function to set this function as the handler for when the XDp_WaitUs driver function is called.

briefdescription.para: This function is used to reset video output for this example.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function will present the resolution of the incoming video stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

simplesect.para: The resolution will be rounded up to the nearest resolution present in the XDpRxSs_VideoTimingModes table.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a video mode chang interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when the power state interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a no video interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a vertical blanking interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a training lost interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a valid video interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a training done interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a bandwidth change interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a training pattern 1 interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a training pattern 2 interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the callback function for when a training pattern 3 interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

briefdescription.para: This function is the main function of the XDp (operating in RX mode) interrupt with timer example.

detaileddescription.para: If the Dprx_SstExample function, which sets up the system succeeds, this function will wait for interrupts.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the interrupt example was unsuccessful - system setup failed.

simplesect.para: Unless setup failed, main will never return since Dprx_SstExample is blocking.

briefdescription.para: Contains a design example using the XDp driver for an DP RX core generated with SST-only functionality.

detaileddescription.para: This example runs the programming sequence for the DP159 retimer. An I2C controller will need to exist in the design have a connection to the DP159. A user-defined hook is used for delay. The reasoning behind this is that MicroBlaze sleep is not very accurate without a hardware timer. For systems that have a hardware timer, the user may override the default MicroBlaze sleep with a function that will use the hardware timer. Also, this example sets up the interrupt controller and defines handlers for various interrupt types. In this way, the RX interrupt handler will arbitrate the different interrupts to their respective interrupt handlers defined in this example. This example will print out the detected resolution of the incoming DisplayPort video stream. This example is meant to take in the incoming DisplayPort video stream and pass it through using the Dprd_Vidpipe* functions which are left for the user to implement.

simplesect.para: This example requires an AXI timer in the system.
For this example to work, the user will need to implement initialization of the system (Dprx_PlatformInit) as this is system-specific.
For this example to display output, the user will need to implement the Dprx_Vidpipe* functions which configure and reset the video pipeline as required as this is system-specific.

In the presence of a DP159 retimer, an I2C controller needs to exist in the design to program it. Depending on how the DP159 connection exists, the user will need to implement Dprx_Dp159Reset function.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 als 01/20/15 Initial creation. 2.0 als 07/07/15 Added DP159 programming. 4.0 als 02/07/16 Added end of line reset for reduced blanking. 5.1 ms 01/23/17 Added xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
